name: Quick-add expense basic flow
description: Build the quick-add UX shortcut for fast expense entry (target: under 3 seconds from open to saved). Frontend-only feature — reuses the existing POST /api/v1/expenses endpoint from feat-017. A globally accessible trigger (floating action button + keyboard shortcut) opens a compact bottom-sheet or modal: amount input focused immediately, source may default to the last used source, category is chosen from the global static catalog defined in feat-016, payer defaults to current user, date defaults to today, and the expense starts as personal/private by default. If the user switches the quick-add entry to household-shared, the flow must ask for an explicit household selection inside quick-add or derive it from the launch surface's local context; it must never depend on a hidden global active-household state. Success shows a brief toast with an Undo link (calls DELETE on the just-created expense within a 5-second window). Per docs/product-specs/quick-add-experience.md. Does NOT include offline queuing or smart ML defaults (post-MVP).
evidence: Implemented global quick-add trigger + dialog in apps/web/src/components/expense/quick-add-expense-trigger.tsx and quick-add-expense-dialog.tsx with session-scoped source defaulting, explicit household validation, current-user payer default for household entries, and 5-second Undo reuse of delete mutation. Code review follow-up on 2026-05-04 added: header trigger button, NoteField and GroupField for household flow, inline network/permission error UX with retry guidance and save-as-private fallback, quick-add timing instrumentation via CustomEvent, suppressCreateErrorToast option in useExpenseForm to avoid duplicate error noise, and targeted tests covering timing, failure recovery paths, and household timing metric. Verified with pnpm --filter web typecheck, pnpm --filter web test -- quick-add-expense-dialog quick-add-expense-trigger, pnpm --filter web lint.
